hero.title: 7 Days, Temples of the South.
hero.titleAccent: The Six Abodes and the Nine Planets.
hero.intro: The ultimate Tamil temple pilgrimage. The six sacred abodes of Lord Murugan combined with the nine planetary temples of the Navagraha, woven into a single seven-day journey through the most sacred geography in the south.
hero.heroImageAlt: Tamil Nadu Murugan and Navagraha temples pilgrimage

overview.heading: 7 Days Arupadai Veedu + Navagraha: The Full Picture
body: This 7 day pilgrimage brings together two of the great Tamil temple circuits: the Arupadai Veedu, the six sacred abodes of Lord Murugan, and the Navagraha, the nine planetary temples around the Cauvery delta. It is the most complete temple pilgrimage we offer in the south.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the journey is designed for devotees who want both circuits in one trip. A knowledgeable driver guide handles the considerable routing and explains the legends, rituals and significance of each temple, from Murugan's hilltop and coastal shrines to the planetary temples of the delta.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Six Abodes of Murugan
items.body: Palani, Swamimalai, Thiruchendur, Thirupparamkundram, Pazhamudircholai and Thiruthani, the sacred Arupadai Veedu of Lord Murugan.

items.title: The Nine Planetary Temples
items.body: The Navagraha temples around Kumbakonam, each dedicated to a celestial body, visited to seek balance and blessings according to one's birth chart.

items.title: The Sacred Cauvery Delta
items.body: The fertile temple belt around Kumbakonam and Thanjavur, dense with ancient Chola shrines and rich tradition, forms the heart of the journey.

days.title: Arrive Madurai
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the Meenakshi Temple and Thirupparamkundram.

days.title: Thiruchendur & Palani
days.description: The coastal abode of Thiruchendur and the hill temple of Palani.

days.title: Swamimalai & Pazhamudircholai
days.description: Two more Murugan abodes en route to the delta.

days.title: Navagraha Temples (Part 1)
days.description: Begin the planetary temple circuit around the delta.

days.title: Thiruthani
days.description: The sixth Murugan abode at Thiruthani.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Two Great Circuits, One Pilgrimage
whyBook.body: Combining the Murugan abodes and the Navagraha temples takes expert routing across Tamil Nadu, and both circuits have their own customs. We handle it all with knowledgeable guides. Dhesu has arranged South India pilgrimages since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Arupadai Veedu & Navagraha Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 7 day pilgrimage is the most complete Tamil temple journey we offer, combining the six abodes of Lord Murugan with the nine Navagraha planetary temples.

Arranged privately, it handles the substantial routing across Tamil Nadu and pairs you with a guide who understands both traditions, making the combined pilgrimage smooth and meaningful.

It is ideal for devotees who want both sacred circuits without making two separate trips.

sections.heading: Two Pilgrimage Traditions in One Trip
sections.body:
The Arupadai Veedu honours Lord Murugan, the Tamil god of war and victory, across six abodes from hilltops to the coast. The Navagraha temples, around the Cauvery delta, are dedicated to the nine celestial bodies of Vedic astrology.

Visiting both in one journey gives a rich, layered experience of Tamil devotion, from the mythology of Murugan to the planetary remedies of the Navagraha.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chennai or Madurai Airport (MAA / IXM). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: November to February is the most comfortable, avoiding the Tamil Nadu heat. Festivals like Thaipusam and Skanda Sashti draw large crowds at Murugan temples. The temples are open year-round, with festival days busiest.

items.question: Do I need to know my birth chart for the Navagraha temples?
items.answer: It helps if you want to focus on specific planetary remedies, but it is not required. Many pilgrims visit all nine temples for overall blessings. Your guide can explain the customary offerings at each shrine.

items.question: How much driving is involved?
items.answer: Both circuits are spread across Tamil Nadu, so there is significant driving. The seven-day pace and private vehicle keep this comfortable, with the route planned to combine the two circuits as efficiently as possible.

cta.heading: Ready for the temple pilgrimage?
